reasoning: `Seq::new(global_tail, f)` produces a sequence of length `global_tail` by definition. Entries beyond `gtail` being dropped is the intended semantics — `interp_log` interprets exactly the committed log prefix up to `gtail`.

Both `simple_log` and `unbounded_log` versions of `compute_nrstate_at_version` return `DT::init_spec()` at version 0. This is the trivially correct base case of the refinement proof — intended by design.

`interp_log` constructs a `Seq` of length `gtail` indexing only `[0, gtail)`. Inserting at index `gtail` doesn't affect any index in that range. This is correct — the interpretation only covers the committed prefix.
